message:
fix(vdbe): clear pending_next_after_delete on Insert to prevent infinite loops

Fix UPDATE statement execution where Delete + Insert sequence could cause
infinite iteration when the rowid doesn't change.

## Problem (br-22iss)
When executing UPDATE (which compiles to Delete followed by Insert on
the same cursor), the pending_next_after_delete flag was not cleared
after Insert. If the new row had the same rowid as the deleted row:
1. Delete sets pending_next_after_delete[cursor_id] = true
2. Insert repositions cursor to the newly inserted row
3. Next checks pending_next_after_delete, skips advancing
4. Column reads the same row again
5. Infinite loop or incorrect row processing

## Solution
Clear pending_next_after_delete entry after Insert opcode execution:
  self.pending_next_after_delete.remove(&cursor_id);

This is safe because Insert repositions the cursor via table_insert(),
which seeks to the correct leaf for the given rowid. The cursor is now
positioned correctly and should advance normally on Next.

## Why this matters for UPDATE
UPDATE compiles to a cursor loop:
  Rewind cursor
  loop:
    Column cursor -> old values
    [evaluate SET expressions]
    [check WHERE]
    Delete cursor          // sets pending_next
    MakeRecord -> record
    Insert cursor rowid record  // NOW clears pending_next
    Next cursor -> loop    // advances correctly

Without this fix, Next might not advance after Insert, causing the
loop to process the same (newly inserted) row repeatedly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
